title: Responsible Person (RP) Duties on a WDA
author: B. Subramanian
excerpt: A senior QP's guide to responsible person duties on a WDA: legal basis, GDP obligations, ICH Q9 risk decisions, contactability and contract RP cover.
cover_image_prompt: A photorealistic clinical-luxury banner of a calm, modern pharmaceutical distribution and quality environment: a temperature-controlled storage area with neatly racked, unbranded medicine cartons softly out of focus in the background, and in the sharp foreground a gloved hand reviewing a clipboard or tablet beside a digital temperature monitor. Cool teal and cyan tones blend into a deep navy backdrop, lifted by a single subtle green accent on a status indicator. Soft, natural side light, shallow depth of field, pristine and authoritative mood, 4:3 aspect ratio, no text, no logos.

faqs.q: Who can act as a Responsible Person on a UK WDA?
faqs.a: The Responsible Person must have appropriate knowledge of, and experience in, wholesale distribution activities and Good Distribution Practice, and be acceptable to the MHRA. They do not have to be a pharmacist, but they must demonstrate the competence, standing and authority to ensure the licence obligations are met. The named individual is assessed by the MHRA during the application and may be questioned directly at inspection.

faqs.q: Can a Responsible Person be a contractor rather than an employee?
faqs.a: Yes. A contract RP is permitted and is common among importers, virtual wholesalers and smaller operators. The key requirement is that the arrangement gives the RP genuine authority, adequate time, and the visibility needed to discharge the role properly. A purely nominal contract that leaves the RP disengaged will not withstand MHRA scrutiny.

faqs.q: What is the difference between an RP and an RPi?
faqs.a: The Responsible Person (RP) oversees Good Distribution Practice activities under a WDA generally. The Responsible Person (import), or RPi, is a separate role specific to the UK, covering the importation of certain medicines from listed countries and confirming that appropriate independent certification is in place. A single business may need both, and they can sometimes be held by the same suitably qualified individual.
